description: Migrate kernel logic from TypeScript to Rust sidecar. Split the service.rs monolith, convert to async, then migrate permissions, filesystem, process management, command resolution, tools, ACP sessions, and SQLite to Rust. TypeScript becomes a thin SDK (~2,500 lines down from ~10,400). See .agent/specs/typescript-to-rust-migration.md for the full design spec.
testPolicy: IMPORTANT: Do NOT run bare `pnpm test` — dev-shell and browser integration tests have pre-existing failures unrelated to this migration. Use scoped test commands specified in each story's acceptanceCriteria. The final story (US-029) addresses full pnpm test green.

userStories.title: Extract state types from service.rs and expand protocol.rs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared state types (VmState, etc.) extracted into a dedicated state.rs module so that service.rs becomes a thin routing layer
acceptanceCriteria: crates/sidecar/src/state.rs exists with all shared state structs/enums moved from service.rs (VmState, session state, configuration types, etc.)
protocol.rs (already exists at 1,353 lines) is expanded if needed with any protocol-related types still in service.rs
service.rs imports from state.rs and protocol.rs instead of defining these types inline
No behavior changes — pure mechanical extraction with identical function signatures
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar passes
userStories.notes: Step 0a — first extraction. Read .agent/specs/typescript-to-rust-migration.md 'Step 0a: Split service.rs'. IMPORTANT: protocol.rs already exists at crates/sidecar/src/protocol.rs (1,353 lines). Do NOT recreate it — only move remaining protocol-related types from service.rs into it. Extract state types first because all domain modules depend on them.

userStories.title: Extract vm.rs from service.rs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want VM lifecycle functions (create_vm, configure_vm, dispose_vm) in a dedicated module
acceptanceCriteria: crates/sidecar/src/vm.rs exists with all VM lifecycle functions moved from service.rs
Includes create_vm, configure_vm, dispose_vm and their helper functions (~1,500 lines)
service.rs delegates VM operations to vm.rs
No behavior changes — pure mechanical extraction
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar passes
userStories.notes: Step 0a. Depends on US-001 (state types must be in state.rs first). VM lifecycle is the top-level entry point for sidecar operations.

userStories.title: Extract bootstrap.rs and bridge.rs from service.rs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want root filesystem construction and host bridge code in dedicated modules
acceptanceCriteria: crates/sidecar/src/bootstrap.rs exists with root filesystem construction and snapshot helpers (~1,000 lines)
crates/sidecar/src/bridge.rs exists with host filesystem access and permission bridge code (~500 lines)
service.rs delegates to these modules
No behavior changes — pure mechanical extraction
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar passes
userStories.notes: Step 0a. Bootstrap handles building the root FS from base layer + overlays. Bridge handles communication with the host Node.js process for filesystem and permission operations.

userStories.title: Extract filesystem.rs from service.rs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all guest filesystem call dispatch handlers in a dedicated module
acceptanceCriteria: crates/sidecar/src/filesystem.rs exists with all guest filesystem dispatch handlers (~1,500 lines)
Includes handlers for readFile, writeFile, mkdir, stat, readdir, symlink, link, chmod, chown, utimes, truncate, rename, delete, exists, realpath, and all other VFS operations
service.rs delegates filesystem RPCs to filesystem.rs
No behavior changes — pure mechanical extraction
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar passes
userStories.notes: Step 0a. Standalone filesystem domain — no cross-cutting dependencies beyond state types.

userStories.title: Extract execution.rs from service.rs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want process spawn, networking, and event pump code in a dedicated module
acceptanceCriteria: crates/sidecar/src/execution.rs exists with process spawn, stdin, kill, networking (TCP/UDP/Unix/DNS), and event pump code (~4,000 lines)
ActiveProcess struct, socket state management, and sync RPC handlers for process/network operations are in execution.rs
Networking stays co-located with execution because ActiveProcess owns socket state
handle_javascript_sync_rpc_request stays in service.rs as the cross-cutting dispatch hub (~700 lines)
service.rs delegates process/networking operations to execution.rs
No behavior changes — pure mechanical extraction
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar passes
userStories.notes: Step 0a. Largest extraction (~4,000 lines) but purely mechanical — cut functions, move to new file, update imports. handle_javascript_sync_rpc_request is the cross-cutting dispatch hub that delegates to domain modules.

s3_plugin.rs moved to plugins/s3.rs

sandbox_agent_plugin.rs moved to plugins/sandbox_agent.rs
Old top-level plugin files deleted

IMPORTANT: These plugin files already exist as top-level files (host_dir_plugin.rs at 796 lines, s3_plugin.rs at 1,439 lines, google_drive_plugin.rs at 1,792 lines, sandbox_agent_plugin.rs at 2,300 lines). This story moves them into a plugins/ subdirectory and adds a shared trait. A js_bridge.rs plugin will be added later.

userStories.title: Move inline service.rs tests to crates/sidecar/tests/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests in separate test files instead of inline #[cfg(test)] blocks
acceptanceCriteria: All #[cfg(test)] mod tests blocks extracted from service.rs and other extracted modules to crates/sidecar/tests/ files
Only trivial unit tests of private helper functions remain inline in source files
Test files organized by domain matching the source modules
All extracted tests pass identically — no test behavior changes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar passes

userStories.title: Convert sidecar main loop from nix::poll to tokio::select!
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the sidecar event loop to use async tokio::select! instead of synchronous nix::poll for proper async wakeup
acceptanceCriteria: The nix::poll loop in stdio.rs (~587 lines) is replaced with a tokio::select! event loop
stdin reading converted to async I/O with the existing NativeFrameCodec
All request handler methods converted to async fn across all domain modules (vm.rs, filesystem.rs, execution.rs, etc.)
Single-task select! model — only one branch runs at a time, &mut self sufficient (no Arc<Mutex>)
Process events received via a tokio channel branch in the select! loop
The existing tokio dependency (used by S3/sandbox agent plugins) extends to the main event loop
nix::poll dependency removed from stdio.rs
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 passes
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/native-sidecar-process.test.ts passes (pre-existing failures acceptable)
userStories.notes: Step 0b. See spec 'Step 0b: Async migration'. The async fn conversion across modules is mechanical (add async keyword + .await). The creative work is rewriting stdio.rs's run() function from sync poll to tokio::select!. Concurrency model stays single-task. IMPORTANT: Do NOT introduce Arc<Mutex> — the select! loop runs one branch at a time.

userStories.title: Restore V8 isolate execution engine with kernel-backed polyfills
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Node.js execution engine to run guest JS/TS inside V8 isolates with kernel-backed polyfills instead of spawning real host node processes. This is the single most critical architectural fix — without it, every guest process escapes the kernel to the real host.
acceptanceCriteria: Guest JS/TS code executes inside V8 isolates managed by the Rust sidecar, NOT in real host node child processes
All Node.js builtins (fs, net, child_process, dns, http, os, etc.) are kernel-backed polyfills that route through the kernel VFS, socket table, and process table
Module loading is fully intercepted — guest require()/import never falls through to real host modules
The sync RPC bridge (SharedArrayBuffer + Atomics.wait or equivalent) connects guest polyfills to the kernel
Guest code cannot observe it is running inside a V8 isolate — process.pid returns kernel PID, fs.readdirSync('/') shows kernel VFS, etc.
Command::new('node') is no longer used for guest code execution in javascript.rs
cargo check -p agent-os-execution passes
cargo test -p agent-os-execution -- --test-threads=1 passes
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL FIX. V8 isolate execution engine restored. The agent-os-v8 binary (crates/v8-runtime/) manages V8 isolates. The execution engine (crates/execution/src/javascript.rs) now spawns V8 isolates instead of real node processes. Polyfills (18K lines) are bundled into crates/execution/assets/v8-bridge.js. IPC protocol (v8_ipc.rs), runtime manager (v8_host.rs), and bridge call translation (v8_runtime.rs) wire polyfill bridge calls to the sidecar's kernel handlers. Internal bridge calls (logging, module resolution, crypto, timers) are handled locally in the event bridge. The sidecar reads entrypoint files from the VFS and passes inline code to avoid module loading bridge calls during synchronous dispatch. Remaining integration: crash_isolation test hangs due to synchronous dispatch model not supporting concurrent bridge call handling — needs the async event loop (US-008's tokio::select!) to properly pump V8 bridge calls.

userStories.title: Add bidirectional frame support to wire protocol and TypeScript client
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the wire protocol to support sidecar-initiated requests so the sidecar can call back into TypeScript
acceptanceCriteria: Protocol has new frame types: sidecar_request (Sidecar->TS) and sidecar_response (TS->Sidecar)
Request ID namespacing: TS-initiated IDs are positive integers, sidecar-initiated IDs are negative integers
Rust sidecar can send sidecar_request frames and receive/correlate sidecar_response frames
TypeScript native-process-client.ts (1,593 lines) updated to parse incoming sidecar_request frames
TypeScript client dispatches sidecar_request to a registered handler callback
TypeScript client sends sidecar_response frames back to sidecar
Existing request/response/event frame handling unchanged
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 passes
pnpm --dir packages/core exec tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/native-sidecar-process.test.ts passes (5 pre-existing failures are acceptable)
userStories.notes: Step 0b continued. See spec 'Wire Protocol' section for SidecarRequestPayload and SidecarResponsePayload enums. native-process-client.ts is at packages/core/src/sidecar/native-process-client.ts (1,593 lines). This bidirectional capability is used by tool invocations (US-020) and JS-bridge mounts (US-012).

userStories.title: Register missing V8 bridge functions, remove dead node_binary() code, verify tests exercise V8 path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all ~80 unregistered bridge functions (networking, crypto, HTTP/2, TLS, dgram) registered on the V8 global so polyfills actually work at runtime, dead Command::new('node') code removed from guest execution paths, and tests verified to exercise the V8 isolate path
acceptanceCriteria: All bridge functions referenced by v8-bridge.js polyfills are registered in SYNC_BRIDGE_FNS/ASYNC_BRIDGE_FNS in crates/v8-runtime/src/session.rs — no undefined globals at runtime
TCP sockets (net.connect, net.createServer), TLS, dgram, HTTP/2, dns, and crypto bridge functions all registered and forwarded to sidecar handlers via map_bridge_method
create_node_child() dead code removed from crates/execution/src/javascript.rs
Prewarm path Command::new(node_binary()) at javascript.rs:879 either removed or clearly gated behind a non-guest-execution flag
Existing cargo test -p agent-os-execution tests verified to exercise the V8 isolate path, not the old node process path
cargo check -p agent-os-v8-runtime passes
cargo check -p agent-os-execution passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 passes
cargo test -p agent-os-execution -- --test-threads=1 passes
userStories.notes: Follow-up to US-008a. Three issues found in audit: (1) ~80 bridge functions (_netSocketConnectRaw, _netServerListenRaw, _dgramSocket*Raw, _networkHttp2*Raw, _crypto*, _tlsGetCiphersRaw, _ptySetRawMode, _batchResolveModules, etc.) exist in v8-bridge.js polyfills and in map_bridge_method/sidecar handlers, but are NOT registered in the V8 session's SYNC_BRIDGE_FNS/ASYNC_BRIDGE_FNS arrays (session.rs lines 743-802). The JS polyfills check typeof === 'undefined' and silently degrade, meaning networking is effectively broken. (2) create_node_child() at javascript.rs:918 is dead code that should be removed. (3) Tests may still exercise the old node path. Check crates/v8-runtime/src/session.rs register_bridge_functions(), crates/execution/src/v8_runtime.rs map_bridge_method(), and crates/sidecar/src/execution.rs for the full forwarding chain.

userStories.title: Implement declarative permissions in Rust and replace TypeScript permission-descriptors.ts

acceptanceCriteria: Rust sidecar evaluates declarative permission rules with glob matching for filesystem paths
Permission domains: fs, network, childProcess, env — each accepts 'allow', 'deny', or structured rules with path/pattern lists
AgentOs.create() in TypeScript accepts declarative permissions object (no callback functions)
agent-os.ts imports updated — no longer imports from permission-descriptors.ts
packages/core/src/sidecar/permission-descriptors.ts (346 lines) deleted
index.ts updated to remove any re-exports from permission-descriptors.ts
Existing tests updated to use declarative permission format
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 passes
pnpm --dir packages/core exec tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/native-sidecar-process.test.ts passes (pre-existing failures acceptable)
userStories.notes: Step 1. This is kernel-level declarative policy (syscall-time checks), NOT ACP interactive permissions. See spec 'Two Permission Systems'. Check what agent-os.ts and index.ts import from permission-descriptors.ts and update those imports.

userStories.title: Add layer management RPCs and bundle base-filesystem.json in sidecar

base-filesystem.json bundled into the sidecar binary via include_bytes! (~13KB)

userStories.title: Add JS-bridge mount plugin for TypeScript VirtualFileSystem callbacks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a sidecar mount plugin that dispatches VFS operations to user-provided TypeScript callbacks via bidirectional frames
acceptanceCriteria: crates/sidecar/src/plugins/js_bridge.rs created
JS-bridge plugin registers at a mount path and intercepts kernel VFS operations under that path
When kernel accesses a JS-bridge path, sidecar pushes JsBridgeCall SidecarRequest to TypeScript
TypeScript dispatches to user's VirtualFileSystem implementation and returns result via JsBridgeResult SidecarResponse
Sidecar holds the kernel operation until the TypeScript result arrives (async, no polling)
Error mapping: generic errors -> EIO, 'not found' -> ENOENT, 'permission denied' -> EACCES, 'already exists' -> EEXIST
Per-call timeout of 30s; on timeout returns EIO
Mount state scoped per-VM
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar passes
userStories.notes: Step 2 (Rust side, part 2). Depends on US-009 (bidirectional frames). See spec 'JS-Bridge Mounts' section. This is the same async callback pattern used by tool invocations — sidecar pushes request, holds, TypeScript runs callback, returns result.

userStories.title: Add process management and shell/terminal RPCs to sidecar

acceptanceCriteria: Sidecar handles process RPCs: WaitProcess (blocking — returns immediately if already exited), ListProcesses, GetProcessTree, GetProcess
Sidecar handles shell RPCs: OpenShell, WriteShell, ResizeShell, CloseShell, ConnectTerminal
Sidecar handles process I/O RPCs: WriteProcessStdin, KillProcess
ProcessOutput and ProcessExited push events use kernel PIDs (not synthetic IDs)

spawn() RPC returns kernel-assigned PID directly
All state scoped per-VM
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar passes
userStories.notes: Step 3 (Rust side). WaitProcess MUST be a blocking RPC — if process already exited, return exit code immediately. This avoids the race condition of event-based waitProcess. See spec 'API Changes'.

userStories.title: Implement JSON-RPC 2.0 NDJSON codec and ACP client in Rust

acceptanceCriteria: crates/sidecar/src/acp/ directory created with mod.rs
JSON-RPC 2.0 NDJSON codec: parse/serialize requests, responses, notifications over newline-delimited JSON
ACP client sends requests and correlates responses with request IDs and configurable timeouts
Permission request deduplication (_seenInboundRequestIds — VM stdout can duplicate NDJSON lines)
Legacy permission method shimming (request/permission vs session/request_permission)
Cancel fallback: send as request first, fall back to notification if agent returns -32601 (method not found)
Permission option normalization (always/allow_always, once/allow_once, reject/reject_once)
Exit drain grace period (50ms delay before rejecting pending requests on agent exit)
Timeout diagnostics (last 20 activity entries included in timeout error messages)
Write Rust tests at crates/sidecar/tests/acp/

cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar passes

ALL edge cases are compatibility workarounds for real agent behaviors — port faithfully from acp-client.ts (564 lines), do NOT simplify or omit any. Read the existing TypeScript acp-client.ts at packages/core/src/acp-client.ts to understand each edge case.

userStories.title: Implement inbound ACP request handling in Rust

Sidecar handles inbound terminal/wait_for_exit — waits for terminal process exit
Sidecar handles inbound terminal/kill — kills terminal process
Sidecar handles inbound terminal/release — releases terminal resources
No round-trip to TypeScript for any inbound request — served directly from sidecar state

userStories.title: Fix V8 bridge dgram/UDP wiring so guest UDP packets reach the host
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the V8 polyfill's dgram bridge to correctly send and receive UDP packets through the kernel socket table so the javascript_dgram_rpc_sends_and_receives_host_udp_packets test passes
acceptanceCriteria: Guest code calling dgram.createSocket/bind/send via V8 polyfill successfully sends packets to host UDP listeners
The _dgramSocketSendRaw bridge call correctly maps through v8_runtime.rs map_bridge_method to the sidecar dgram.send handler
The sidecar dgram handler receives the bridge call, sends the packet via the kernel socket table, and returns the result to the V8 isolate
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar --test service javascript_dgram_rpc_sends_and_receives_host_udp_packets -- --test-threads=1 passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 passes (no dgram-related regressions)
userStories.notes: Known blocker from US-009a work. The V8 polyfill sends _dgramSocketSendRaw which maps to dgram.send via map_bridge_method. The sidecar has full dgram handlers in execution.rs (lines 516-584). The bug is in the wiring — either the sync RPC response isn't reaching the V8 isolate correctly, or the guest-to-host port mapping is broken. Debug the full chain: V8 polyfill → _dgramSocketSendRaw → v8_ipc → v8_host event bridge → JavascriptSyncRpcRequest → sidecar execution.rs dgram handler → UdpSocket::send_to → response back to V8. Check crates/execution/assets/v8-bridge.js dgram section, crates/execution/src/v8_runtime.rs map_bridge_method, and crates/sidecar/src/execution.rs dgram handlers.

userStories.title: Fix all remaining test failures and get full pnpm test green

acceptanceCriteria: pnpm check-types passes across all workspace packages
pnpm test passes across all workspace packages (zero failures)
pnpm build passes
pnpm lint passes
All pre-existing test failures from dev-shell, browser, and native-sidecar-process resolved
No test files skipped or disabled to pass — all tests run and pass
userStories.notes: Final cleanup story. Known pre-existing failures to fix: (1) packages/dev-shell — 8 integration test failures (kernel.spawn node returns exit code 1, @xterm/headless CJS/ESM import issue on Node ≥ 22), (2) packages/core/tests/native-sidecar-process.test.ts — 4 failures (listener/UDP queries, socketTable/processTable state, connectTerminal, openShell), (3) packages/browser — test failures. Debug each systematically — these are runtime/platform issues, not migration regressions.

userStories.title: Fix timer scheduling so setTimeout/setInterval actually fire
userStories.description: As a developer, I want timers to work in V8 guest code. Currently _scheduleTimer is a stub returning 0 — timers are created but never fire.
acceptanceCriteria: _scheduleTimer async bridge call in javascript.rs event bridge creates a real timer (tokio::time::sleep or channel-based) that fires after the requested delay
Timer expiration sends a StreamCallback or BridgeResponse back to V8 to resolve the pending promise
The V8 session event loop (session.rs run_event_loop) receives and dispatches timer events
setTimeout, setInterval, and clearTimeout work correctly in guest code
Local _loadPolyfill kernelTimerCreate/kernelTimerArm/kernelTimerClear handlers coordinate with the async timer mechanism
cargo check -p agent-os-execution passes
cargo test -p agent-os-execution -- --test-threads=1 timer passes
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL — setTimeout/setInterval are broken, which breaks all async guest code. The _scheduleTimer bridge function is async (in ASYNC_BRIDGE_FNS). The LocalBridgeState already handles kernelTimerCreate/kernelTimerArm/kernelTimerClear via _loadPolyfill dispatch. The gap is that _scheduleTimer itself returns 0 immediately and never fires. Need to wire timer state between the local bridge (which tracks timer IDs) and the async event bridge (which can schedule delayed events). The V8 session event loop already handles StreamCallback messages — use that for timer delivery.

userStories.title: Wire stdin reads to kernel process stdin
userStories.description: As a developer, I want guest code to read from stdin so interactive agent sessions work. Currently _kernelStdinRead returns null.
acceptanceCriteria: _kernelStdinRead in LocalBridgeState forwarded to sidecar via SyncRpcRequest instead of returning null
Sidecar handles __kernel_stdin_read by reading from the process's kernel stdin buffer
If no stdin data is available, the call blocks up to the requested timeout then returns null
Guest process.stdin.read() and readline work correctly
cargo check -p agent-os-execution passes
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 stdin passes
userStories.notes: Currently handled locally as a stub in javascript.rs LocalBridgeState (line ~1991). Should be forwarded to sidecar instead. _kernelStdinRead is in ASYNC_BRIDGE_FNS. The sidecar already handles WriteProcessStdin — this is the read counterpart.

userStories.title: Implement crypto basic handlers (randomFill, randomUUID, hash, hmac, pbkdf2, scrypt)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want basic crypto operations to work in guest code. Currently randomFill uses timestamps (INSECURE) and randomUUID is not RFC 4122. The other 4 methods have no handler.
acceptanceCriteria: _cryptoRandomFill in LocalBridgeState uses a CSPRNG (getrandom or rand crate) instead of timestamp-based XOR
_cryptoRandomUUID generates proper RFC 4122 v4 UUIDs
crypto.hashDigest sidecar handler added — supports sha256, sha512, md5, sha1 at minimum
crypto.hmacDigest sidecar handler added
crypto.pbkdf2 sidecar handler added
crypto.scrypt sidecar handler added
Match arms added to service_javascript_sync_rpc dispatch in execution.rs
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 crypto passes
cargo test -p agent-os-execution -- --test-threads=1 crypto passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL for agents — API auth uses HMAC/hashing. randomFill (javascript.rs:1964) and randomUUID (javascript.rs:1976) are LOCAL stubs in LocalBridgeState — fix in-place with proper crypto. The other 4 methods fall through to sidecar as crypto.hashDigest etc. via map_bridge_method but the sidecar has NO crypto.* match arms. Add a new service_javascript_crypto_sync_rpc handler in execution.rs. Use ring or openssl crate for implementations. Reference: .agent/recovery/secure-exec/nodejs/src/bridge-handlers.ts (6,405 lines).

userStories.title: Implement crypto advanced handlers (ciphers, keys, signatures, DH, subtle)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want advanced crypto operations (encryption, signing, key generation, DH, WebCrypto) to work in guest code.
acceptanceCriteria: crypto.cipheriv, crypto.decipheriv, crypto.cipherivCreate, crypto.cipherivUpdate, crypto.cipherivFinal handlers added (5 methods)
crypto.sign, crypto.verify, crypto.asymmetricOp handlers added (3 methods)
crypto.createKeyObject, crypto.generateKeyPairSync, crypto.generateKeySync, crypto.generatePrimeSync handlers added (4 methods)
crypto.diffieHellman, crypto.diffieHellmanGroup, crypto.diffieHellmanSessionCreate, crypto.diffieHellmanSessionCall handlers added (4 methods)
crypto.subtle handler added (WebCrypto API — 1 method)
All 17 methods dispatched via service_javascript_crypto_sync_rpc in execution.rs
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 crypto passes
userStories.notes: Lower priority than US-032 (basic crypto). Same pattern — add match arms to the crypto handler. 17 methods total. The cipheriv family uses stateful cipher contexts (create → update → final). Key operations need RSA/EC/Ed25519 support. crypto.subtle is the WebCrypto API subset. Reference: .agent/recovery/secure-exec/nodejs/src/bridge-handlers.ts.

userStories.title: Add TCP socket advanced ops to sidecar dispatch
userStories.description: As a developer, I want socket read, setNoDelay, setKeepAlive, waitConnect, and server accept to work so TCP servers/clients behave correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: net.socket_read match arm added to service_javascript_net_sync_rpc — reads available data from a connected socket
net.socket_set_no_delay match arm added — sets TCP_NODELAY on socket
net.socket_set_keep_alive match arm added — sets SO_KEEPALIVE on socket
net.socket_wait_connect match arm added — blocks until socket connection completes or timeout
net.server_accept match arm added — accepts next pending connection from a listening server
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 net passes
userStories.notes: The sidecar already handles net.connect, net.listen, net.poll, net.write, net.shutdown, net.destroy, net.server_poll, net.server_connections, net.server_close in service_javascript_net_sync_rpc (execution.rs:4008-4016). These 5 methods are mapped by map_bridge_method in v8_runtime.rs but missing from the sidecar match arms. Same function, just new match arms.

userStories.title: Add TLS support to sidecar dispatch
userStories.description: As a developer, I want TLS upgrade, client hello inspection, TLS query, and cipher listing to work so guest code can make HTTPS connections.
acceptanceCriteria: net.socket_upgrade_tls match arm added — upgrades a TCP socket to TLS using rustls or native-tls
net.socket_get_tls_client_hello match arm added — returns parsed TLS ClientHello for SNI inspection
net.socket_tls_query match arm added — returns TLS session info (cipher, protocol, peer cert)
tls.get_ciphers match arm added — returns list of supported TLS cipher suites
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 tls passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-034 (TCP advanced). These 4 methods are mapped in v8_runtime.rs but have no sidecar handler. TLS upgrade wraps an existing connected socket with a TLS layer. Use rustls or native-tls. The sidecar needs to track TLS state per socket. Reference: .agent/recovery/secure-exec/nodejs/src/bridge/network.ts (11,149 lines).

userStories.title: Add HTTP/1 server request/respond handlers to sidecar
userStories.description: As a developer, I want guest HTTP servers to handle incoming requests and send responses so agents can serve tools over HTTP.
acceptanceCriteria: net.http_request match arm added — dispatches an outbound HTTP request through the sidecar's network adapter
net.http_respond match arm added — sends an HTTP response on a server connection
Guest http.createServer() can accept connections and respond
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 http passes
userStories.notes: 3 methods mapped in v8_runtime.rs but no sidecar handler. The sidecar already handles net.listen (for creating TCP listeners) and net.fetch (for outbound requests). These handlers bridge the gap for HTTP-level semantics on top of the TCP primitives. Reference: .agent/recovery/secure-exec/nodejs/src/bridge/network.ts.

userStories.title: Add HTTP/2 full stack to sidecar
userStories.description: As a developer, I want HTTP/2 server, session, and stream operations to work in guest code.
acceptanceCriteria: New service_javascript_http2_sync_rpc handler function added to execution.rs
Server ops: net.http2_server_listen, net.http2_server_poll, net.http2_server_close, net.http2_server_respond (4 methods)

Session ops: net.http2_session_connect, net.http2_session_request, net.http2_session_settings, net.http2_session_set_local_window_size, net.http2_session_goaway, net.http2_session_close, net.http2_session_destroy, net.http2_session_poll, net.http2_session_wait (9 methods)

Stream ops: net.http2_stream_respond, net.http2_stream_push_stream, net.http2_stream_write, net.http2_stream_end, net.http2_stream_close, net.http2_stream_pause, net.http2_stream_resume, net.http2_stream_respond_with_file (8 methods)
All 21 methods dispatched from service_javascript_sync_rpc match block
HTTP/2 state (sessions, streams) tracked per-process in ActiveProcess
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 http2 passes
userStories.notes: Lowest priority networking story — most agents use HTTP/1.1. 21 methods total, all mapped in v8_runtime.rs but with no sidecar handler. Use h2 crate for HTTP/2 framing. This is the largest single networking story. Can be deferred if agents don't need HTTP/2. Reference: .agent/recovery/secure-exec/nodejs/src/bridge/network.ts.

userStories.title: Add upgrade socket and dgram extras to sidecar
userStories.description: As a developer, I want WebSocket upgrade sockets and UDP metadata operations to work.
acceptanceCriteria: net.upgrade_socket_write, net.upgrade_socket_end, net.upgrade_socket_destroy match arms added (3 methods)
dgram.address match arm added — returns bound address/port of a UDP socket
dgram.setBufferSize match arm added — sets send/recv buffer size
dgram.getBufferSize match arm added — gets current buffer size

userStories.notes: 6 small handlers. Upgrade sockets are for HTTP upgrade (WebSocket). The sidecar already handles dgram.createSocket/bind/send/poll/close — these 3 extras are missing from the match arms in service_javascript_dgram_sync_rpc. Reference: execution.rs service_javascript_dgram_sync_rpc.

userStories.title: Wire PTY raw mode to kernel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want _ptySetRawMode to actually toggle raw mode on the kernel PTY so interactive shells work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: _ptySetRawMode removed from LocalBridgeState stub and forwarded to sidecar as __pty_set_raw_mode
Sidecar handles __pty_set_raw_mode by calling kernel PTY manager to toggle raw/cooked mode
Guest process.stdin.setRawMode(true) works correctly
cargo check -p agent-os-sidecar passes
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 pty passes
userStories.notes: 1 method. Currently a no-op stub in LocalBridgeState (javascript.rs:1992). The kernel already has PTY management with line discipline — this just needs to toggle it. Mapped to __pty_set_raw_mode in v8_runtime.rs.

userStories.title: Triage and fix failing execution tests for V8 path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the execution test suite green so CI catches regressions. ~40 tests fail because they were written for the old Command::new('node') path.
acceptanceCriteria: Every test in cargo test -p agent-os-execution categorized as: (a) delete — tests old loader/node_import_cache mechanics irrelevant to V8, (b) fix — tests real behavior but with wrong assertions for V8 bridge, (c) keep — already passes on V8 path
Deleted tests documented in a comment block listing what was removed and why
Fixed tests have V8-compatible assertions (bridge call flow instead of loader hook flow)
No tests use or assume Command::new('node') execution path
cargo test -p agent-os-execution -- --test-threads=1 passes with 0 failures
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 passes
userStories.notes: The ~40 failures fall into patterns: (1) ~15 test loader mechanics (node_import_cache.rs template generation, ESM hook injection, runner.mjs materialization) — these are dead code for V8 and should be deleted, (2) ~25 test real behavior (fs, child_process, env hardening) but assert on node process artifacts (exit codes, stderr formats, env vars) that differ under V8 bridge — fix assertions. NEVER restore Command::new('node') to make tests pass.

userStories.title: Run Pi agent end-to-end in V8 isolates
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to boot a Pi agent session inside V8 isolates and verify it completes a real task end-to-end. None of the current stories test the full agent lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Pi agent boots via createSession('pi') with the V8 execution engine (not host node)
Agent receives a prompt, makes tool calls (filesystem, child_process), and returns a response
ACP JSON-RPC session lifecycle works: session/start → task/start → events → task/finish
All bridge calls (fs, child_process, crypto, timers, stdin) exercised during the session
Test uses @copilotkit/llmock with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='mock-key' — no real API tokens
pnpm --dir packages/core exec vitest run tests/pi-headless.test.ts passes
userStories.notes: End-to-end agent validation. This is the real integration test — everything else tests subsystems in isolation. Depends on bridge gap stories (US-030-040) being complete. Use the existing pi-headless.test.ts as the starting point. The test should verify the agent sees kernel VFS (not host fs), kernel PIDs (not host PIDs), and kernel env (not host env).

userStories.title: V8 isolate startup time benchmark
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to measure cold and warm V8 session creation latency to ensure it meets performance targets.
acceptanceCriteria: Benchmark script measures cold V8 session creation (first session after v8-runtime binary starts)
Benchmark script measures warm V8 session creation (subsequent sessions reusing the running v8-runtime)
Benchmark measures time from CreateSession IPC to first BridgeCall received
Results logged with p50/p95/p99 over 10+ iterations
If cold start >500ms, investigate V8 startup snapshot prewarming
Benchmark added to scripts/benchmarks/ and runnable via a simple command
userStories.notes: Performance validation. The V8 runtime (crates/v8-runtime/) supports snapshot-based session creation via snapshot.rs. Cold start includes V8 isolate creation + v8-bridge.js evaluation. Warm start reuses the snapshot. Measure both. Compare against the old Command::new('node') path baseline if available.

userStories.title: Memory baseline for V8 vs old node process
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to compare RSS per agent session between V8 isolates and the old host node process to validate the memory improvement.
acceptanceCriteria: Benchmark measures RSS of the v8-runtime binary with 0, 1, 5, and 10 concurrent sessions
Marginal RSS per session calculated (delta between N and N+1 sessions)
Results compared against baseline of old node process path (if available, otherwise document V8-only numbers)
Benchmark added to scripts/benchmarks/ and runnable via a simple command
Results include breakdown: v8-runtime base RSS, per-session delta, v8-bridge.js evaluation overhead
userStories.notes: Performance validation. The whole point of V8 isolates was lower memory per session vs spawning full node processes. Use process RSS from /proc/[pid]/status or similar. The existing scripts/benchmarks/pi-entrypoint-memory.bench.ts may be a useful reference for measurement patterns.

userStories.title: Guest escape audit — automated isolation invariant tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an automated test suite that verifies no guest code can reach host APIs, leak host state, or escape the kernel isolation boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Test suite in crates/sidecar/tests/isolation_audit.rs or crates/execution/tests/isolation_audit.rs
Tests verify: guest process.env does not contain AGENT_OS_* vars or host env
Tests verify: guest process.pid returns kernel PID, not host PID
Tests verify: guest fs.readdirSync('/') shows kernel VFS root, not host root
Tests verify: guest process.cwd() returns kernel CWD, not host path
Tests verify: guest require('fs') returns kernel-backed polyfill, not real node:fs
Tests verify: guest cannot access real host network (no real TCP connections)
Tests verify: guest error stack traces do not reveal host filesystem paths
Tests verify: guest require.resolve() returns guest-visible paths
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar --test isolation_audit -- --test-threads=1 passes
userStories.notes: Security hardening. The CLAUDE.md rules (crates/CLAUDE.md invariants 1-8) are clear but there's no automated test suite that systematically checks them. Each test should run guest JS code via the V8 engine and assert on the output. Reference: crates/execution/CLAUDE.md 'Virtualization Invariants' section.

userStories.title: Port process.env virtualization to V8 bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I want guest process.env to show only kernel-provided environment variables, not host env. The old bridge/process.ts had full env virtualization.
acceptanceCriteria: Guest process.env returns only the env vars configured via the kernel, not host process.env
AGENT_OS_* internal control variables are stripped from guest process.env
NODE_OPTIONS, LD_PRELOAD, DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES stripped from guest env
Guest process.env modifications are isolated per-process (don't affect other guests or host)
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 env passes
cargo test -p agent-os-execution -- --test-threads=1 env passes
userStories.notes: The old secure-exec had full env virtualization in bridge/process.ts (2,251 lines). The V8 bridge (v8-bridge.js) may already partially handle this via the process polyfill, but it needs verification. Check what guest_env filtering the sidecar does in service.rs and whether the V8 runtime exposes host env to guest code. Reference: .agent/recovery/secure-exec/nodejs/src/bridge/process.ts.

userStories.title: Port signal handling to V8 bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I want guest process.on('SIGTERM', ...) to route through the kernel signal table so signal delivery works correctly in V8 isolates.
acceptanceCriteria: Guest process.on('SIGTERM', handler) registers with the kernel signal table, not host signal handlers
Kernel signal delivery (from kill() syscall or parent process) triggers guest signal handlers
SIGINT, SIGTERM, SIGHUP, SIGWINCH supported at minimum
Guest process.kill(pid, signal) routes through kernel process table
Signal state tracked per-process in kernel process table (not per-V8-isolate)
cargo test -p agent-os-sidecar -- --test-threads=1 signal passes
userStories.notes: The kernel already has full signal support in process_table.rs (SIGCHLD, SIGTERM, SIGWINCH, etc.). The gap is wiring guest process.on('SIGTERM') through the V8 bridge to the kernel signal table. The old secure-exec bridge/process.ts had this. The V8 bridge needs a signal registration bridge call and the sidecar needs to deliver signal events to the V8 session.

userStories.title: CI pipeline for V8 runtime binary
userStories.description: As a developer, I want crates/v8-runtime/ built and tested in CI so regressions are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: CI workflow builds crates/v8-runtime/ (cargo build -p agent-os-v8-runtime)
CI workflow runs crates/v8-runtime/ tests (cargo test -p agent-os-v8-runtime -- --test-threads=1)
CI workflow runs crates/execution/ tests that exercise V8 (cargo test -p agent-os-execution -- --test-threads=1)
V8 binary artifact is cached between CI runs to avoid rebuilding rusty_v8 from source
CI passes on the current branch
userStories.notes: Operational. crates/v8-runtime/ uses v8 = '130' (rusty_v8) which takes ~5 minutes to build from source. CI should cache the built binary and the rusty_v8 build artifacts. Check if there's an existing CI workflow (.github/workflows/) to extend rather than creating a new one.
